# UC-14.1.12 · Firmware Version Compliance Across Fleet

## Description

Known-bad IOS/NX-OS/IOS-XE builds expose the network to CVEs; compliance reporting supports change windows.

## Value

Known-bad IOS/NX-OS/IOS-XE builds expose the network to CVEs; compliance reporting supports change windows.

## Implementation

Poll ENTITY-MIB / vendor firmware revision OIDs on a weekly schedule. Maintain CSV of approved builds per platform. Drive remediation tickets for non-compliant devices.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP TA (ENTITY-MIB, vendor OIDs), SolarWinds/Prime export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="snmp:inventory"` (sysDescr, entPhysicalFirmwareRev).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll ENTITY-MIB / vendor firmware revision OIDs on a weekly schedule. Maintain CSV of approved builds per platform. Drive remediation tickets for non-compliant devices.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="snmp:inventory"
| stats latest(firmware_version) as fw by device_name, model
| lookup approved_network_firmware.csv model OUTPUT approved_fw
| where fw!=approved_fw
| table device_name, model, fw, approved_fw
```

Understanding this SPL

**Firmware Version Compliance Across Fleet** — Known-bad IOS/NX-OS/IOS-XE builds expose the network to CVEs; compliance reporting supports change windows.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="snmp:inventory"` (sysDescr, entPhysicalFirmwareRev). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP TA (ENTITY-MIB, vendor OIDs), SolarWinds/Prime export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: snmp:inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="snmp:inventory". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by device_name, model** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where fw!=approved_fw` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Firmware Version Compliance Across Fleet**): table device_name, model, fw, approved_fw


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant devices), Pie chart (compliance %), Bar chart (by site).

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="snmp:inventory"
| stats latest(firmware_version) as fw by device_name, model
| lookup approved_network_firmware.csv model OUTPUT approved_fw
| where fw!=approved_fw
| table device_name, model, fw, approved_fw
```

## Visualization

Table (non-compliant devices), Pie chart (compliance %), Bar chart (by site).
